articles.headline: Fourth World Cup still has South Korea captain Son Heung-Min feeling 'like a kid'
articles.description: South Korea captain Son Heung-min says he feels like a "kid" as he enters his fourth World Cup, where he will debut against the Czech Republic in a Group A match in Guadalajara on Thursday.

images.name: Son Heung-Min [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min was all smiles on Wednesday at South Korea's final training session before their 2026 FIFA World Cup opener against Czechia.
images.credit: Ulises RUIZ / AFP via Getty Images

articles.headline: Will 2026 prove to be Son Heung-Min's final FIFA World Cup hurrah?
articles.description: Son Heung-Min's previous three World Cups perfectly mirrored where he was in his career. So what does 2026 hold for him?

images.name: Will the 2026 World Cup be Son Heung-Min's last hurrah? [1500x844]
Son Heung-Min 2014 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's first World Cup goal came in South Korea's ill-fated 2014 campaign -- where they picked up just one point from three games in their worst showing since 1998.
images.credit: ADRIAN DENNIS / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2018 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's memorable sealer in a stunning 2-0 win over Germany in 2018 ultimately proved in vain as South Korea narrowly failed to make it out of the group stage.

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2022 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: South Korea finally reached the round of 16 at the 2022 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 12 years -- although that was where their campaign would end with a 4-1 loss to Brazil.

images.name: Will Mexico or South Korea top Group A at the World Cup?
images.caption: Gab & Juls debate who will win Group A between Mexico, South Korea, Czechia and South Africa at the FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Japan have a plan to win the World Cup by 2050. How close will they get in 2026?
articles.description: Japan's "100 Year Vision" is well ahead of schedule, but are the Samurai Blue ready win the World Cup now?

images.name: Japan World Cup chances [1500x844]
images.alt: Wataru Endo, Takefusa Kubo and Ritsu Doan

images.name: Wataru Endo [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was sidelined by injury in February, Wataru Endo has recovered in time for this third FIFA World Cup -- and first as captain of Japan.

images.name: Japan vs. Brazil [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup saw them claim friendly victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.name: Pochettino: USMNT are moving in a good direction ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

categories.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - AFC/Concacaf Playoff

league.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - AFC/Concacaf Playoff

categories.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - AFC/CONMEBOL Playoff

league.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - AFC/CONMEBOL Playoff

categories.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - Concacaf/OFC Playoff

league.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - Concacaf/OFC Playoff

articles.headline: Midfield metronome Hwang In-Beom could be key for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Hwang In-Beom is not South Korea's most-prominent name. But he could be their most important player at the World Cup.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was initially a doubt after picking up an injury in March, Hwang In-Beom has been passed fit to feature for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup -- where he will likely play a key role for them in midfield.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Having had plenty of previous experience in Europe with Rubin Kazan, Olympiacos and Red Star Belgrade, Hwang In-Beom has now spent the past two seasons in the Eredivisie with Feyenoord.

images.name: Son Heung-min set for fourth World Cup with South Korea
images.caption: South Korea name their 26-man squad for the 2026 World Cup, headlined by a fourth competition appearance for captain Son Heung-min.

articles.headline: Why Japan could be the most exciting team at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Even with a couple of key players out through injury, Japan could still be the most exciting team at the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Japan [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup has seen them record victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever over the past seven months.

images.name: Kaoru Mitoma [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan were dealt a real blow ahead of the World Cup after injuy ruled out Kaoru Mitoma, who scored the only goal of the game when they beat England 1-0 at Wembley back in March.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
